frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I understand why women are cautious, but sometimes the conversation becomes so broad that every man is treated like a suspect by default. In cities like Mumbai, plenty of decent men are just trying to get through their day, and it’s unfair to act as though basic trust itself is unreasonable. We can talk about women’s safety without implying that all men are potential threats.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it recenters concern onto men’s feelings and reputations in response to women’s safety fears, rather than engaging the structural issue. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal, generalization policing, and the demand that women perform trust toward male strangers.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 34. Married with one child aged three. A woman working as a journalist in Mumbai. You are on a panel at a media industry event. A male journalist on the same panel is asked about his biggest professional achievement this year. He describes a long investigation he published. He is not asked about his child. You are asked next about your biggest professional achievement this year. Before you can answer the moderator adds Ñ and of course as a working mother how do you balance it all. You are responding to this question in front of 300 people. What you say and what you do not say and the smile you maintain while saying it is the entire content of this persona.
